top_signals.title: SoftBank Seeks Stake in Japan’s Top Utility to Power AI Boom
top_signals.summary: SoftBank Group Corp. is looking to invest in Japan’s biggest power utility to help secure the electricity needed to expand in artificial intelligence, the company’s chief executive officer said.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Andy Burnham will be ‘in hock’ to the bond markets whether he likes it or not
top_signals.summary: With the highest gilt yields in the G7, more borrowing is no longer an option for the next Prime Minister. That leaves the traditional options: raise taxes, cut spending or embark on more ambitious supply-side reforms in areas such as planning, says Daniel Mahoney Governments needing money usually have three options: tax more, spend less, [...]

top_signals.title: EU Rides Out Budget Spat Without Spooking Markets or Ratings
top_signals.summary: The European Union’s regime for monitoring public finances has arguably just passed its toughest health test since a revamp after the pandemic.

top_signals.title: India’s Shapoorji Pallonji Group Seeks Fresh Bond Extension as Refinancing Drags
top_signals.summary: India’s Shapoorji Pallonji Group has offered bondholders a 30-basis point fee in exchange for approving another extension on debt owed by its unit, according to people familiar with the matter.

top_signals.title: What if Andy Burnham had become Labour leader in 2015?
top_signals.summary: On his third try, Andy Burnham looks all but certain to become Labour leader. But what if he had taken the reins of the party eleven years earlier? Matt Kenyon imagines an alternative timeline…  2015 It’s a world that looks a lot like our own. There’s just one, seemingly minor, difference: A cluster of well-meaning [...]
